title: QIZ Security Raises $17M Seed Round to Scale Enterprise Cryptographic Posture Platforms - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Post-quantum cryptography orchestrator QIZ Security raised a $17M seed co-led by Bessemer and Merlin Ventures to scale an agentless platform that continuously discovers and remediates enterprise cryptographic assets, riding tightening federal 'Q-Day' migration deadlines and already deployed at 100+ organizations.

insights.claim: QIZ Security raised a $17 million seed round co-led by Bessemer Venture Partners and Merlin Ventures, with participation from Evolution Equity Partners, Qbeat Ventures, Singtel Innov8, and Qino Cyber Capital.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Tier-1 cyber investors leading a PQC seed signals institutional conviction that crypto-migration is an imminent enterprise spend category, not a distant research problem.
insights.quote: The investment was co-led by Bessemer Venture Partners and Merlin Ventures, with cross-border participation from Evolution Equity Partners, Qbeat Ventures, Singtel Innov8, and Qino Cyber Capital.

insights.claim: The raise is timed to tightening federal deadlines mandating immediate preparation for 'Q-Day,' the point at which fault-tolerant quantum hardware can crack legacy public-key encryption.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Regulatory deadlines convert PQC from optional to compliance-mandatory spend, creating a policy-driven demand tailwind for the whole sub-sector.
insights.quote: the future cryptographic flashpoint where fault-tolerant quantum hardware will possess the processing capacity to crack legacy public-key encryption layers
insights.locator: The capital injection accelerates product feature extensions

insights.claim: Enterprises are exposed because legacy RSA and ECC cryptography is baked into unmapped webs of third-party code, database connections, and communication links, creating harvest-now-decrypt-later risk for critical infrastructure and financial networks.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The 'you can't protect what you can't see' framing is the core sales wedge for cryptographic-discovery vendors; harvest-now-decrypt-later makes the threat present-tense even before Q-Day.
insights.quote: This exposes companies to harvest-now, decrypt-later data extraction strategies conducted by malicious actors

insights.claim: QIZ built an agentless, API-based integration platform that continuously discovers on-prem, cloud, and hybrid cryptographic assets, maps them to business software, flags weak ciphers and expired certificates, and ranks vulnerabilities by impact to build automated remediation roadmaps.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Agentless discovery avoids the performance drag of host agents/packet sniffers, a differentiator that lowers deployment friction for large enterprises.
insights.quote: Rather than relying on intrusive, network-heavy host agents or network packet sniffer probes that degrade data center performance, the company built an agentless, API-based integration platform.

insights.claim: The platform enables 'crypto-agility'—swapping vulnerable classical algorithms for quantum-resistant variants across multi-vendor hybrid nodes without resetting the underlying corporate code stack.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Non-disruptive algorithm swapping is the key value proposition that makes migration feasible at enterprise scale, defining the category's winning feature.

insights.claim: QIZ was co-founded in 2025 by Ben Volkow (CEO), Lenny Ridel (CTO), and Dr. Itan Barmes (CSO), who previously directed Deloitte's Global Quantum Cyber Readiness Team overseeing PQC migrations for banks, telecoms, and healthcare.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A founder pulled from Deloitte's quantum-readiness practice brings a warm enterprise pipeline and credibility, a common enterprise-sales advantage.
insights.locator: The company was co-founded in 2025 by industry veterans

insights.claim: The platform aligns with NIST PQC lattice-based standards, CNSA 2.0 timelines, and the EU's DORA and NIS2 mandates, and integrates with Google Cloud, AWS, IBM, CrowdStrike, Cisco, Deloitte, and EY, with 100+ organizations already using it.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Broad hyperscaler/advisory integrations plus 100+ live customers this early indicate real go-to-market traction and channel leverage.
insights.quote: These integrations allow the 100+ organizations already utilizing the platform to achieve complete crypto-agility
